description_long: This object is a vintage handheld electronic game, featuring a rectangular plastic body with a slightly raised front panel. The front face displays a monochrome LCD screen with a racing game graphic, surrounded by a metallic-looking bezel and labeled in Cyrillic script, suggesting it is a Soviet-era 'Elektronika' game. Four red physical buttons are arranged on the front, two on each side, likely for game control and function selection. To the right of the screen, there are several small indicator lights and additional labeling. The back of the device is plain, adorned with two stickers: one reading 'PUNK Rock' and another 'Vans OFF THE WALL.' The sides are thin and show the device's compact, portable form factor.
description: A vintage handheld electronic game with a metallic front, monochrome LCD screen, red control buttons, and punk-themed stickers on the back.
description_view_0: Front view showing the screen, red control buttons, indicator lights, and Cyrillic branding.
description_view_1: Side profile revealing the device's slim, rectangular shape and the protrusion of the front panel.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain surface with 'PUNK Rock' and 'Vans OFF THE WALL' stickers.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again highlighting the device's thinness and button placement.
